title: Doctor-Oriented Professional UI with Glass Effects & Dynamic Section Visibility

requirements.text: Hide any public-facing section (About, Clinics, Services, Social Media) entirely when it has no content. A section is considered empty if it has no items (for list-based sections) or no meaningful text/image (for single-content sections). Empty sections must be invisible to visitors — no placeholder, no skeleton, no empty card grid.

quotes: Keep sections empty. Until I add content keep them invisible.

acceptanceCriteria: About section is hidden when bio text is empty and no profile image is set.
Clinics section is hidden when the clinics list is empty.
Services section is hidden when the services list is empty.
Social Media section is hidden when the social links list is empty.
Each section renders normally as soon as at least one piece of content is present.
No empty state message, skeleton, or placeholder is shown to public visitors.

requirements.text: Redesign the overall public-facing UI and admin interface with a professional, doctor-oriented aesthetic. Apply a clean medical-grade design system: crisp white and deep charcoal tones with a trusted clinical accent (e.g., teal or soft emerald), refined typography using Space Grotesk for headings and Inter for body text, precise spacing, and subtle micro-interactions. The design must convey authority, precision, and trust — appropriate for a medical professional's digital presence.

quotes: Make the hightech UI more professional doctor oriented. Smart UI.

acceptanceCriteria: Color palette reflects a medical/clinical aesthetic (no purely decorative neon overuse).
Typography hierarchy is clear with Space Grotesk headings and Inter body text.
All interactive elements have polished hover and focus states.
Layout feels structured, professional, and appropriate for a doctor's profile site.
The overall design communicates expertise and trust.

requirements.text: Apply glassmorphism (frosted glass) effects consistently across cards, modals, navigation, and panel surfaces throughout both the public site and the admin dashboard. Glass panels should use semi-transparent backgrounds with backdrop blur, a subtle light border, and a soft inner glow. Ensure legibility of text on all glass surfaces in both light and dark contexts.
acceptanceCriteria: GlassCard component is updated to use backdrop-blur and semi-transparent background.
Admin sidebar and panel surfaces use consistent glass styling.
Navigation header uses a frosted glass effect.
All glass surfaces maintain readable text contrast.
Glass effects are applied consistently — no mix of opaque and glass cards within the same section.

requirements.text: Redesign the AdminDashboard into a powerful, smart admin interface. The sidebar should be structured with clear section groupings and icons, display active state clearly, and feel like a professional CMS control panel. The content area should use card-based editors with clear visual hierarchy, inline status indicators (saved, unsaved changes), and section health indicators showing which sections are currently empty vs. populated.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin sidebar has grouped navigation items with icons for each section.
Active section is clearly highlighted in the sidebar.
Each editor section shows a status badge indicating whether the section is empty or has content.
Editors use glass card containers with clear section titles and action buttons.
Save/cancel actions have clear visual states (loading, success, error).
The admin interface feels like a professional medical practice management tool.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All backend data models and API endpoints must remain unchanged.
Section visibility logic must be purely frontend — no backend changes required.
Glass effects must not break accessibility or text contrast standards.
nonGoals: Adding new backend data fields or endpoints.
Adding new section types not already present.
